time/CLOCK_BOOTTIME.name: CLOCK_BOOTTIME
time/CLOCK_BOOTTIME.description: Identical to CLOCK_MONOTONIC, except it also includes any time that the system is suspended. This allows applications to get a suspend-aware monotonic clock without having to deal with the complications of CLOCK_REALTIME, which may have discontinuities if the time is changed using settimeofday() or similar. Availability: Linux >= 2.6.39. Added in version 3.7.

time/CLOCK_HIGHRES.name: CLOCK_HIGHRES
time/CLOCK_HIGHRES.description: The Solaris OS has a CLOCK_HIGHRES timer that attempts to use an optimal hardware source, and may give close to nanosecond resolution. CLOCK_HIGHRES is the nonadjustable, high-resolution clock. Availability: Solaris. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: The Solaris OS has a CLOCK_HIGHRES timer that attempts to use an optimal hardware source, and may give close to nanosecond resolution. CLOCK_HIGHRES is the nonadjustable, high-resolution clock. Availa...

time/CLOCK_MONOTONIC.name: CLOCK_MONOTONIC
time/CLOCK_MONOTONIC.description: Clock that cannot be set and represents monotonic time since some unspecified starting point. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Clock that cannot be set and represents monotonic time since some unspecified starting point. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

time/CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW.name: CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW
time/CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW.description: Similar to CLOCK_MONOTONIC, but provides access to a raw hardware-based time that is not subject to NTP adjustments. Availability: Linux >= 2.6.28, macOS >= 10.12. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Similar to CLOCK_MONOTONIC, but provides access to a raw hardware-based time that is not subject to NTP adjustments. Availability: Linux >= 2.6.28, macOS >= 10.12. Added in version 3.3.

time/CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW_APPROX.name: CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW_APPROX
time/CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW_APPROX.description: Similar to CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, but reads a value cached by the system at context switch and hence has less accuracy. Availability: macOS >= 10.12. Added in version 3.13.

info.excerpt: Similar to CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW, but reads a value cached by the system at context switch and hence has less accuracy. Availability: macOS >= 10.12. Added in version 3.13.

time/CLOCK_PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID.name: CLOCK_PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID
time/CLOCK_PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID.description: High-resolution per-process timer from the CPU. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: High-resolution per-process timer from the CPU. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

time/CLOCK_PROF.name: CLOCK_PROF
time/CLOCK_PROF.description: High-resolution per-process timer from the CPU. Availability: FreeBSD, NetBSD >= 7, OpenBSD. Added in version 3.7.

info.excerpt: High-resolution per-process timer from the CPU. Availability: FreeBSD, NetBSD >= 7, OpenBSD. Added in version 3.7.

time/CLOCK_REALTIME.name: CLOCK_REALTIME
time/CLOCK_REALTIME.description: Real-time clock. Setting this clock requires appropriate privileges. The clock is the same for all processes. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Real-time clock. Setting this clock requires appropriate privileges. The clock is the same for all processes. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

time/CLOCK_TAI.name: CLOCK_TAI
time/CLOCK_TAI.description: International Atomic Time The system must have a current leap second table in order for this to give the correct answer. PTP or NTP software can maintain a leap second table. Availability: Linux. Added in version 3.9.

info.excerpt: International Atomic Time The system must have a current leap second table in order for this to give the correct answer. PTP or NTP software can maintain a leap second table. Availability: Linux. Adde...

time/CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID.name: CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID
time/CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID.description: Thread-specific CPU-time clock. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Thread-specific CPU-time clock. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

time/CLOCK_UPTIME.name: CLOCK_UPTIME
time/CLOCK_UPTIME.description: Time whose absolute value is the time the system has been running and not suspended, providing accurate uptime measurement, both absolute and interval. Availability: FreeBSD, OpenBSD >= 5.5. Added in version 3.7.

info.excerpt: Time whose absolute value is the time the system has been running and not suspended, providing accurate uptime measurement, both absolute and interval. Availability: FreeBSD, OpenBSD >= 5.5. Added in ...

time/CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW.name: CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW
time/CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW.description: Clock that increments monotonically, tracking the time since an arbitrary point, unaffected by frequency or time adjustments and not incremented while the system is asleep. Availability: macOS >= 10.12. Added in version 3.8.

info.excerpt: Clock that increments monotonically, tracking the time since an arbitrary point, unaffected by frequency or time adjustments and not incremented while the system is asleep. Availability: macOS >= 10.1...

time/CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW_APPROX.name: CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW_APPROX
time/CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW_APPROX.description: Like CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW, but the value is cached by the system at context switches and therefore has less accuracy. Availability: macOS >= 10.12. Added in version 3.13.

info.excerpt: Like CLOCK_UPTIME_RAW, but the value is cached by the system at context switches and therefore has less accuracy. Availability: macOS >= 10.12. Added in version 3.13.

time/clock_getres.name: clock_getres
time/clock_getres.description: Return the resolution (precision) of the specified clock clk_id. Refer to Clock ID Constants for a list of accepted values for clk_id. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Return the resolution (precision) of the specified clock clk_id. Refer to Clock ID Constants for a list of accepted values for clk_id. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

time/clock_gettime.name: clock_gettime
time/clock_gettime.description: Return the time of the specified clock clk_id. Refer to Clock ID Constants for a list of accepted values for clk_id. Use clock_gettime_ns() to avoid the precision loss caused by the float type. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Return the time of the specified clock clk_id. Refer to Clock ID Constants for a list of accepted values for clk_id. Use clock_gettime_ns() to avoid the precision loss caused by the float type. Availa...

time/clock_gettime_ns.name: clock_gettime_ns
time/clock_gettime_ns.description: Similar to clock_gettime() but return time as nanoseconds. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.7.

info.excerpt: Similar to clock_gettime() but return time as nanoseconds. Availability: Unix. Added in version 3.7.

time/clock_settime.name: clock_settime
time/clock_settime.description: Set the time of the specified clock clk_id. Currently, CLOCK_REALTIME is the only accepted value for clk_id. Use clock_settime_ns() to avoid the precision loss caused by the float type. Availability: Unix, not Android, not iOS. Added in version 3.3.

info.excerpt: Set the time of the specified clock clk_id. Currently, CLOCK_REALTIME is the only accepted value for clk_id. Use clock_settime_ns() to avoid the precision loss caused by the float type. Availability: ...

time/clock_settime_ns.name: clock_settime_ns
time/clock_settime_ns.description: Similar to clock_settime() but set time with nanoseconds. Availability: Unix, not Android, not iOS. Added in version 3.7.

info.excerpt: Similar to clock_settime() but set time with nanoseconds. Availability: Unix, not Android, not iOS. Added in version 3.7.

time/ctime.name: ctime
time/ctime.description: Convert a time expressed in seconds since the epoch to a string of a form: 'Sun Jun 20 23:21:05 1993' representing local time. The day field is two characters long and is space padded if the day is a single digit, e.g.: 'Wed Jun 9 04:26:40 1993'. If secs is not provided or None, the current time as returned by time() is used. ctime(secs) is equivalent to asctime(localtime(secs)). Locale information is not used by ctime().
